narrative:
On July 3, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 42.5%, with 1,017 cases heard out of 2,394 scheduled across 58 judges. This performance reflects a decline from the previous day's hearing rate of 49.3% (1,197 of 2,430 cases heard) but aligns closely with the 42.8% rate observed on July 1. 

Workloads and efficiency varied significantly among individual benches. Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the day's heaviest docket, hearing 94 out of 244 scheduled cases (38.5%). In contrast, Justice Shivashankar Amarannavar in Hall 20 achieved high throughput by hearing 53 of his 63 scheduled matters (84.1%). Justice V. Srishananda in Hall 21 also posted a strong disposal performance, hearing 64 of 81 scheduled cases (79.0%).

In terms of procedural stages, Orders dominated the docket with 815 scheduled matters, yielding a 47.0% hearing rate, down from the 56.2% recorded on July 2. Final Hearings saw 105 of 359 scheduled cases heard (29.2%), showing a marginal rise from the previous day's 25.1%. 

Case type analysis reveals that Writ Petitions (WP) constituted the largest category with 1,000 scheduled cases, converting at a rate of 37.0%—a drop from yesterday's 47.6%. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) saw 135 of 291 cases heard (46.4%), while Regular Second Appeals (RSA) achieved a high conversion rate of 67.4%, with 93 of 138 scheduled cases heard.
